name: Isaac TeSlaa
headline: Red-zone touchdown magnet eyeing Detroit's vacant No. 3 role
body:
TeSlaa's rookie line is a study in efficiency without volume. On just 27 targets and a 4.9% target share across 14 games, he turned 16 catches into 239 yards and six touchdowns, posting a robust 0.66 EPA per play and a 55.6% success rate. The usage was deliberately downfield and concentrated near the goal line: 22 air yards per reception, 11 red-zone looks and three end-zone targets. The touchdown rate won't hold at six scores on 16 grabs, but the role is real.

The path forward hinges on snaps, not talent. He played under 40% of them while St. Brown and Jameson Williams soaked up the targets, yet Kalif Raymond's exit opens the No. 3 job for a near-6-foot-4 vertical threat. Projections around 25 catches, 350 yards and four scores frame him as a late-round dynasty stash and bench upside, one injury ahead of weekly WR2 relevance.
